skill.name: javascript-pro
skill.description: Guide modern JavaScript work across ES6+, async control flow, Node.js APIs, browser compatibility, testing, and performance tuning.
skill.summary: Improve modern JavaScript with async patterns, runtime compatibility, error handling, tests, and performance guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all three static findings as false positives in plain instructional text. No prompt injection, command execution, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line describes JavaScript, ES6+, async patterns, and Node.js APIs. No cryptographic algorithm, crypto API use, credential handling, or executable behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain descriptive metadata for a JavaScript skill. There is no reference to DES, MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or any code that could perform cryptographic work.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 33, which lists ES6+ features such as destructuring, modules, and classes. This is documentation content, not a weak cryptographic algorithm or implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a JavaScript feature list, and the likely trigger is a substring inside ordinary programming terminology. No cryptographic use or dangerous implementation pattern is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 45, which advises avoiding callback-heavy code with modern patterns. The file contains no commands, host inspection, process listing, environment probing, or system discovery instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is ordinary JavaScript style guidance. I found no evidence of reconnaissance intent, shell usage, filesystem probing, network access, or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: JavaScript teams often need help with async behavior, runtime differences, and maintainable ES6+ patterns. This skill guides implementation, debugging, testing, and optimization for Node.js and browser code.
actual_capabilities: Guides modern JavaScript implementation using ES6+ language features.
Helps debug promises, async/await behavior, event loops, and race conditions.
Advises on Node.js APIs, browser APIs, module structure, and compatibility constraints.
Recommends robust error handling at appropriate boundaries.
Produces testing guidance for async behavior with Jest patterns.
Considers performance profiling and bundle size for browser projects.
limitations: It does not provide full backend architecture decisions beyond JavaScript implementation concerns.
It is not focused on non-JavaScript runtimes or language ecosystems.
It can discuss TypeScript migration, but it is not a dedicated TypeScript architecture skill.
It needs project runtime targets and compatibility requirements for precise recommendations.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy JavaScript
use_cases.description: Refactor older JavaScript into clearer ES6+ patterns while preserving behavior and browser support.

use_cases.title: Debug Async Production Issues
use_cases.description: Analyze promise chains, async/await flows, event loop timing, and race conditions in Node.js services.

use_cases.title: Improve Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Create practical Jest test strategies for asynchronous functions, modules, and error paths.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an Async Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of JavaScript control flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how this JavaScript async function runs, including promise resolution, error handling, and any event loop behavior I should know.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor to Modern JavaScript
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this JavaScript to ES6+ style while keeping the same behavior. Note browser or Node.js compatibility concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Race Condition
prompt_templates.prompt: Find possible race conditions in this JavaScript flow. Recommend safer async patterns, boundary error handling, and tests that prove the fix.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Runtime and Bundle Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this JavaScript for runtime performance and browser bundle impact. Suggest measurable improvements and any compatibility tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: A promise chain fails silently after one branch rejects.
output: A clear explanation of where rejection handling is missing.
A safer async/await structure with boundary-level error handling described in prose.
A Jest testing plan that covers success, rejection, and timing behavior.

output_examples.input: A browser module needs to support modern bundlers and older target browsers.
output: A compatibility assessment for language features and browser APIs.
A module structure recommendation with clean exports and documented constraints.
A concise polyfill and bundle-size strategy.

output_examples.input: A Node.js utility has unclear performance behavior under load.
output: A review of likely bottlenecks and async scheduling issues.
A profiling plan with measurable checkpoints.
Implementation guidance focused on error boundaries and maintainability.

best_practices: Share runtime targets, browser support requirements, and module system constraints before asking for implementation advice.
Ask for tests together with async refactors so behavior stays verifiable.
Request compatibility and bundle-size notes when code will run in browsers.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for broad backend architecture choices unrelated to JavaScript implementation details.
Do not ask for TypeScript-only design guidance when type-system architecture is the main concern.
Do not omit runtime context when asking about Node.js, browser APIs, modules, or polyfills.

faq.question: Does this skill write JavaScript code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide modern JavaScript implementation, refactoring, async handling, tests, and compatibility decisions.

faq.question: Can it help with Node.js and browser code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers both Node.js APIs and browser APIs, including compatibility and module structure concerns.

faq.question: Is this a TypeScript skill?
faq.answer: No. It can discuss migration and type safety, but its main focus is JavaScript.

faq.question: Can it debug async behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for promises, async/await, event loop behavior, and race condition prevention.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest Jest test patterns for asynchronous JavaScript and error paths.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains instructional JavaScript guidance and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
